title: Passing metric/evaluate timeout as an environment variable
author: icrustandi
body:
**Describe the bug**
Docs mention that configuration values can be passed in as env variables using key-value pairs. This works fine for top-level configs like interval and logVerbosity, but how would this work with the timeout parameter for evaluate and metric methods? Should the value just be a nested json?

**To Reproduce**
In the yaml:
```
config:
    - name: interval
      value: "10000"
    - name: logInterval
      value: "3"
    - name: metric
      value: "{"type": "shell", "timeout": 2500, "shell": {"entrypoint": "python", "logStderr": true}"
  
```

**Expected behavior**
Override metric and evaluate timeout through env variables

**Kubernetes Details (`kubectl version`):**
v1.25.14

title: Kubernetes Metrics CPU Util returning raw numbers
author: icrustandi

title: Distribute arm64 binary
author: skeggse
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

The `.tar.gz` distributes only an `amd64` binary.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I'd love to have both `amd64` and `arm64` binaries distributed in the releases.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Build it myself.

**Additional context**

Could imagine also building images containing the binary, but at the very least distributing multiple architectures would be helpful!

title: plural for resource always adds "s"
author: martin-ilavsky
body:
**Describe the bug**

`kindPlural := fmt.Sprintf("%ss", strings.ToLower(kind))`
This always adds 's' to kind. But in case of CRD resource like logstash, the plural is logstashes and not logstashs. This is causing it to fail

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Deploy logstash CRD
2. Use scaleTargetRef  as '{"kind": "Logstash", "name": "logstash", "apiVersion": "logstash.k8s.elastic.co/v1alpha1"}'
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
find correct resource

**Kubernetes Details (`kubectl version`):**
1.32

title: [question] Does this repo only support kubernetes metrics?
author: Jeffwan
body: I am curious whether this custom pod autoscaler framework support fetching metrics from pod directly instead of using kubernetes metrics?

title: Update to k8shorizmetrics/v3 and log partial K8s metric server errors
author: jthomperoo
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
From discussion on https://github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/discussions/192

Partial errors from the K8s metrics API are swallowed and not logged.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Partial errors should be logged to help with debugging.

This is supported by [k8shorizmetrics/v3](https://github.com/jthomperoo/k8shorizmetrics/releases/tag/v3.0.0).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**
N/A

title: Metrics with type http brings the whole deployment config as query
author: lyudmilalala
body:
**Kubernetes Details (`kubectl version`):** K8s in Docker on Windows 10 K8s version v1.27.2 **Bug & Reproduce** I try the [get metrics by http request example](https://github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/tree/master/example/http-request). It works properly if everything are unchanged. However, if I add more features into the `deployment.yaml`, make it as below ```yaml apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: as-test-deploy namespace: as-test-ns spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: hello-kubernetes template: metadata: labels: app: hello-kubernetes spec: containers: - name: hello-kubernetes image: paulbouwer/hello-kubernetes:1.5 ports: - containerPort: 8080 protocol: TCP env: - name: NODE_NAME valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: spec.nodeName - name: POD_NAME valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: metadata.name - name: POD_NAMESPACE valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: metadata.namespace - name: POD_IP valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: status.podIP resources: requests: memory: "600Mi" limits: memory: "600Mi" readinessProbe: tcpSocket: port: 8080 initialDelaySeconds: 5 periodSeconds: 5 failureThreshold: 3 ``` CPA will give out the error ``` E0919 06:15:47.113456 1 main.go:277] HTTP request failed, status: [414], response: ' 414 Request-URI Too Long

Request-URI Too Long

The requested URL's length exceeds the capacity limit for this server.

Apache/2.4.25 (Debian) Server at w2a.random.org Port 443
' ``` I think it is because the deployment configuration is appended as a query parameter of the request. This conjecture can be proved by another line of error log mentioned below. Currently, for workaround, I write a python shell script, and send requests there. **Expected behavior** The `deployment.yaml` used in production will probably be longer than the one I list above, so I think we should avoid to take it in query, or at least give users a choice not to send it by query. **Additional context - The other error log** If everything are unchanged, the following error will be printed after running CPA for around 10 minutes. I think it is caused by frequestly request to [https://www.random.org/integers/](https://www.random.org/integers/), and won't regard it as a client-side issue. ``` E0919 05:49:39.365893 1 main.go:277] Get "https://www.random.org/integers/?base=10&col=1&format=plain&max=5&min=1&num=1&rnd=new&value=%7B%22resource%22%3A%7B%22kind%22%3A%22Deployment%22%2C%22apiVersion%22%3A%22apps%2Fv1%22%2C%22metadata%22%3A%7B%22name%22%3A%22as-test-deploy%22%2C%22namespace%22%3A%22as-test-ns%22%2C%22uid%22%3A%2231b899b6-978e-4cb6-89ec-a20d7a7a1651%22%2C%22resourceVersion%22%3A%222092030%22%2C%22generation%22%3A3%2C%22creationTimestamp%22%3A%222023-09-19T05%3A47%3A14Z%22%2C%22annotations%22%3A%7B%22deployment.kubernetes.io%2Frevision%22%3A%223%22%2C%22kubectl.kubernetes.io%2Flast-applied-configuration%22%3A%22%7B%5C%22apiVersion%5C%22%3A%5C%22apps%2Fv1%5C%22%2C%5C%22kind%5C%22%3A%5C%22Deployment%5C%22%2C%5C%22metadata%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22annotations%5C%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-deploy%5C%22%2C%5C%22namespace%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-ns%5C%22%7D%2C%5C%22spec%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22replicas%5C%22%3A1%2C%5C%22selector%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22matchLabels%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22app%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-app%5C%22%7D%7D%2C%5C%22template%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22metadata%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22annotations%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22controller.kubernetes.io%2Fpod-deletion-cost%5C%22%3A%5C%221%5C%22%7D%2C%5C%22labels%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22app%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-app%5C%22%7D%7D%2C%5C%22spec%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22containers%5C%22%3A%5B%7B%5C%22env%5C%22%3A%5B%7B%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22POD_NAME%5C%22%2C%5C%22valueFrom%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22fieldRef%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22fieldPath%5C%22%3A%5C%22metadata.name%5C%22%7D%7D%7D%2C%7B%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22POD_NAMESPACE%5C%22%2C%5C%22valueFrom%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22fieldRef%5C%22%3A%7B%5C%22fieldPath%5C%22%3A%5C%22metadata.namespace%5C%22%7D%7D%7D%5D%2C%5C%22image%5C%22%3A%5C%22lyudmilalala%2Fflask-test-img%3A1.0.0%5C%22%2C%5C%22imagePullPolicy%5C%22%3A%5C%22IfNotPresent%5C%22%2C%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-pod%5C%22%2C%5C%22ports%5C%22%3A%5B%7B%5C%22containerPort%5C%22%3A8080%7D%5D%7D%5D%2C%5C%22serviceAccountName%5C%22%3A%5C%22app-admin%5C%22%7D%7D%7D%7D%5Cn%22%7D%2C%22managedFields%22%3A%5B%7B%22manager%22%3A%22kubectl-client-side-apply%22%2C%22operation%22%3A%22Update%22%2C%22apiVersion%22%3A%22apps%2Fv1%22%2C%22time%22%3A%222023-09-19T05%3A49%3A19Z%22%2C%22fieldsType%22%3A%22FieldsV1%22%2C%22fieldsV1%22%3A%7B%22f%3Ametadata%22%3A%7B%22f%3Aannotations%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Akubectl.kubernetes.io%2Flast-applied-configuration%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Aspec%22%3A%7B%22f%3AprogressDeadlineSeconds%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Areplicas%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3ArevisionHistoryLimit%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aselector%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Astrategy%22%3A%7B%22f%3ArollingUpdate%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AmaxSurge%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AmaxUnavailable%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Atype%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Atemplate%22%3A%7B%22f%3Ametadata%22%3A%7B%22f%3Aannotations%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Acontroller.kubernetes.io%2Fpod-deletion-cost%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Alabels%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aapp%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Aspec%22%3A%7B%22f%3Acontainers%22%3A%7B%22k%3A%7B%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22as-test-pod%5C%22%7D%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aenv%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22k%3A%7B%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22POD_NAME%5C%22%7D%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aname%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AvalueFrom%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AfieldRef%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%2C%22k%3A%7B%5C%22name%5C%22%3A%5C%22POD_NAMESPACE%5C%22%7D%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aname%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AvalueFrom%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AfieldRef%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Aimage%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AimagePullPolicy%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aname%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aports%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22k%3A%7B%5C%22containerPort%5C%22%3A8080%2C%5C%22protocol%5C%22%3A%5C%22TCP%5C%22%7D%22%3A%7B%22.%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AcontainerPort%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3Aprotocol%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%2C%22f%3Aresources%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AterminationMessagePath%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3AterminationMessagePolicy%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D%2C%22f%3AdnsPolicy%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f%3ArestartPolicy%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22f context deadline exceeded ```

title: Node example
author: AllanOricil
body: Could you add an example with Node? Thank you

title: Add step to edit pod annotation controller.kubernetes.io/pod-deletion-cost before scaling.
author: LQBing
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

The different pods of an application could have different utilization levels. On scale down, the application may prefer to remove the pods with lower utilization. This works if the application itself controls the down scaling; for example, the driver pod of a Spark deployment.

Kubernetes v1.22 added a new annotation controller.kubernetes.io/pod-deletion-cost` for pod. But I don't know how to add a step to reorder pod-deletion-cost in CPA.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Adding a step allows me to customize the sorting pod-deletion-cost action before scaling.

**Additional context**

https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/replicaset/#pod-deletion-cost

https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/labels-annotations-taints/#pod-deletion-cost

title: Target multiple deployments in one custom auto scaler
author: mohammadkhavari
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have a microservice and want to create a custom autoscaler for services, considering I want to use the same scaling policy for all services, by using CPA I should make multiple CPA's for scaling services because I can target just one deployment by name on CPA, in the end, there's multiple CPA pods. I think It's not an efficient solution.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
one CPA pod targets multiple deployments and runs (metric gatherer, evaluation) for each of them on the interval (in some order).

title: Refactor to use jthomperoo/k8shorizmetrics for K8s metric gathering
author: jthomperoo
body: The new <https://github.com/jthomperoo/k8shorizmetrics> project should be used K8s metric gathering instead of the internal code in this project.

title: Enrich CPA Custom Resource object with status, events, annotations for the scaling decisions
author: StefanCenusa
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Almost every time a value is modified on the CPA custom resource, the CPA Pod is recreated. Although in most cases this is not a problem, sometimes this affects scaling because the downscale stabilisation period is reset. For example, if a user modifies the CPA's min/max replicas, the CPA Pod will be recreated and most likely the replica count will be updated to the one computed on startup, rather than considering previous values (within the stabilisation window).
Moreover, I think that sending events or updating status and/or annotations of the CPA CR (as HPA does on its resources) would be nice for debugging/inspecting scaling using CPA.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Ideally, CPA should do this (updating CPA CR status / annotations / sending events) in the background after the evaluation stage was ran. There is also the solution to manually call the kubernetes api from within the CPA Pod at the end of the evaluation. This gives the user greater flexibility, but it take some more work / is more error prone. In order to have at least this last solution, the CPA Operator should add some more permissions to the Role that it creates for the CPA Pod

title: Client Certificate Support
author: WatcherWhale
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Add the ability to use client certificates, to authorize with in the http method for metric gathering.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I want to specify a path where a `client.crt,` `client.key` and a `ca.crt` to use  in the HTTP request.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An alternative could be to use a Kubernetes TLS secret. 

**Additional context**
Here is an example of how to do it in GO https://smallstep.com/hello-mtls/doc/client/go.

title: Can't use go install due to replace directives in go.mod
author: dmorneau
body:
**Describe the bug**
go install does not work with custom-pod-autoscaler because its go.mod includes replace directives

**To Reproduce**

`go install github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/v2@v2.4.0`

**Expected behavior**
It would be useful if the package was compatible with go install (for context, I'm trying to install it in an existing container).

**Additional context**
```
go: downloading github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/v2 v2.4.0
go install: github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/v2@v2.4.0 (in github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/v2@v2.4.0):
	The go.mod file for the module providing named packages contains one or
	more replace directives. It must not contain directives that would cause
	it to be interpreted differently than if it were the main module.
```

https://github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/blob/7c8f66d125c3cffcbfdf76665219f2cb70cdb89f/go.mod#L18

title: Support  operators (CRD based custom controllers)
author: ghost
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Today CPA does not work with operators.  instead, it keeps logging error "Error while autoscaling: failed to get managed resource: the server could not find the requested resource"

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Do what kubernetes HPA does; instead of querying replicas from a resource like replcationcontroller etc. retrieve scale object
(have a look at scaleForResourceMappings) 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
see above

title: "per-resource" run mode queries kubernetes metrics with wrong labelSelector
author: StefanCenusa
body:
**Describe the bug**

I've run into an issue when trying to write a custom-pod-autoscaler that uses kubernetes metrics.
The CPA logged `Error while autoscaling: failed to get metrics: failed to get required Kubernetes metrics: invalid metrics (1 invalid out of 1), first error is: failed to get resource metric: unable to get metrics for resource cpu: no metrics returned from resource metrics API` although metrics were available for that resource. It only worked after I've added `labels` on my deployment resource that matched the pod labels.

I think this issue is caused by [this code](https://github.com/jthomperoo/custom-pod-autoscaler/blob/8f2cd13c7db2969964d65c8fd57eb34f6bfbfcaa/internal/k8smetricget/k8smetricget.go#L115) where CPA tries to get metrics based on the resource's labels instead of the ones defined in `spec.selector` of a Deployment or a StatefulSet.
```
metric, err := c.getMetric(currentReplicas, spec, namespace, labels.Set(resource.GetLabels()).AsSelector())
```

In my opinion, this behaviour is unwanted, because there are cases where Deployments could have different labels than pods they own. Thus, querying for Pods' metrics with labelSelector built from Deployment's labels could lead to no metrics being returned. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Deploy a Deployment with no labels defined
```
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: hello-kubernetes
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: hello-kubernetes
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: hello-kubernetes
    spec:
      containers:
 ...
```
2. Create a CPA that uses kubernetes metrics; use `runMode: "per-resource"`
3. Target the CPA to this deployment
```
...
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: hello-kubernetes
  roleRequiresMetricsServer: true
...
```
4. Enabled verbose logs on CPA
5. See error

**Expected behavior**
CPA to query for metrics based on Pods of the resource, not the resource itself when it is a Deployment or StatefulSet.

title: Add context to error messages
author: jthomperoo
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Debugging can be difficult because the error is propagated up and logged out with minimal information, instead error wrapping/unwrapping should be used to add more information.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Use error wrapping/unwrapping to add context to errors.

title: Support Argo Rollouts
author: jthomperoo
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The Custom Pod Autoscaler would be better if it could provide support for Argo Rollouts.
https://argoproj.github.io/argo-rollouts/

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Argo Rollouts should be supported in the same way that core Kubernetes resources such as a Deployment or ReplicaSet is supported.

**Additional context**
This was raised as part of a feature request for the [predictive-horizontal-pod-autoscaler](https://github.com/jthomperoo/predictive-horizontal-pod-autoscaler) as part of issue jthomperoo/predictive-horizontal-pod-autoscaler#52.

title: Create a Java/SpringBoot example
author: jthomperoo
body: An example similar to the ones for Python and Golang should be added for Java to act as a quick start/reference for Java developers.

title: Metrics gathered for more than 1 statefulsets are not in correct order
author: prayagsingh
body:
**Describe the bug**
I'm trying to create a custom-autoscaler for jibri(a component used for recording in [jitsi](https://github.com/jitsi/docker-jitsi-meet)) based on the stats it offers. Jibri offers the below stats.
```
When recording is OFF.
{ "busyStatus": "IDLE", "count": "1", "available": "1" }

When recording is ON
{ "busyStatus": "BUSY", "count": "1", "available": "0" }
```

***JIbri is deployed as a StatefulSets***

***Scenario: 1***
--> when running a single jibri instance
 I started the recording and jibri `busyStatus` is `BUSY` and then it scales the jibri as expected.

****log snippet scenario 1****
```
I0609 16:54:16.305490       1 metricget.go:163] Attempting to gather metrics for each pod
I0609 16:54:16.306096       1 metricget.go:177] Running metric gathering for pod: jibri-0
I0609 16:54:16.459031       1 metricget.go:182] Metric gathered: { "busyStatus": "BUSY", "count": "1", "available": "0" }
I0609 16:54:16.459089       1 metricget.go:190] All metrics gathered for each pod successfully
I0609 16:54:16.459109       1 autoscaler.go:63] Retrieved metrics: [0xc0009143c0]
I0609 16:54:16.459133       1 autoscaler.go:65] Attempting to evaluate metrics
I0609 16:54:16.459148       1 evaluatecalc.go:46] Evaluating gathered metrics
I0609 16:54:16.459909       1 evaluatecalc.go:63] Attempting to run evaluation logic
I0609 16:54:16.499594       1 evaluatecalc.go:68] Evaluation determined: {"targetReplicas": 2}
I0609 16:54:16.499620       1 evaluatecalc.go:70] Attempting to parse evaluation
I0609 16:54:16.499642       1 evaluatecalc.go:76] Evaluation parsed: &{TargetReplicas:2}
I0609 16:54:16.499659       1 autoscaler.go:74] Metrics evaluated: &{TargetReplicas:2}
I0609 16:54:16.499665       1 autoscaler.go:76] Attempting to scale resource based on evaluation
I0609 16:54:16.499687       1 scaling.go:62] Determining replica count for resource 'jibri'
I0609 16:54:16.499694       1 scaling.go:71] Replica count determined: 1
I0609 16:54:16.499703       1 scaling.go:81] Scale target set to 1 replicas
I0609 16:54:16.499712       1 scaling.go:118] Picked max evaluation over stabilization window of 20 seconds; replicas 2
I0609 16:54:16.499912       1 scaling.go:141] Rescaling from 1 to 2 replicas
I0609 16:54:16.499933       1 scaling.go:142] Attempting to parse group version
I0609 16:54:16.499943       1 scaling.go:148] Group version parsed: apps/v1
I0609 16:54:16.499954       1 scaling.go:155] Attempting to get scale subresource for managed resource
I0609 16:54:16.513506       1 scaling.go:160] Scale subresource retrieved: &Scale{ObjectMeta:{jibri  jitsi  b6339f9f-1193-4737-b161-413d87839057 17877105 0 2021-06-07 05:01:52 +0000 UTC <nil> <nil> map[] map[] [] []  []},Spec:ScaleSpec{Replicas:1,},Status:ScaleStatus{Replicas:1,Selector:k8s-app=jibri,scope=jitsi,},}
I0609 16:54:16.513689       1 scaling.go:162] Attempting to apply scaling changes to resource
I0609 16:54:16.533012       1 scaling.go:168] Application of scale successful
I0609 16:54:16.533053       1 autoscaler.go:90] Scaled resource successfully
```

***Scenario: 2***
Once scenario one is completed then I have multiple jibri instances i.e `jibri-0` and `jibri-1`. The status of `jibri-0` is still `BUSY` and while the  `jibri-1` is `IDLE` since I'm still using `jibri-0` for recording. Now the real problem occurs. Auto-scaler starts showing the jibri stats in the wrong order. Please find below the logs.

****log snippet scenario 2****
```
I0609 16:55:46.302799       1 metricget.go:163] Attempting to gather metrics for each pod
I0609 16:55:46.303097       1 metricget.go:177] Running metric gathering for pod: jibri-0
I0609 16:55:46.934170       1 metricget.go:182] Metric gathered: { "busyStatus": "IDLE", "count": "2", "available": "1" }
I0609 16:55:46.934653       1 metricget.go:177] Running metric gathering for pod: jibri-1
I0609 16:55:47.103815       1 metricget.go:182] Metric gathered: { "busyStatus": "BUSY", "count": "2", "available": "1" }
I0609 16:55:47.103857       1 metricget.go:190] All metrics gathered for each pod successfully
```
I cross verified these stats at container level and above stats are in wrong order. for `jibri-0` , `busyStatus` should be `BUSY` and for `jibri-1` it should be `IDLE`.


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Deploy the entire jitsi stack with jibri as a sts.
2. Now deploy the custom-autoscaler
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
The order of the stats should be in correct order.

**Kubernetes Details (`kubectl version`):**
Kubernetes version client: v1.19.6
Kubernetes version server: v1.20.7+IKS

**Additional context**
**config files**

***cpa.yaml***

```
apiVersion: custompodautoscaler.com/v1
kind: CustomPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: jibri-autoscaler
  namespace: jitsi
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jibri-autoscaler
        image: prayagsingh/jibri-autoscaler:v1.6.4
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: StatefulSet
    name: jibri
  # Below config is extending config.yaml file. Configuration set as an environment variable takes precedence over
  #  configuration set in a configuration file. All configuration options defined as key-value pairs in the config YAML
  #   are converted into environment variables by the operator  
  config:
    # Interval defines in milliseconds how frequently the autoscaler should run.
    # The autoscaler will run, then wait this many milliseconds before running again
    - name: interval
      value: "30000"
    - name: maxReplicas
      value: "3"
    - name: minReplicas
      value: "1"
    # 0 for normal
    # 1 for verbose
    # 2 - more verbose around high level logic such as autoscaling/rest api
    # 3 - more verbose around lower level logic such as metric gathering and evaluation  
    - name: logVerbosity
      value: "3"
    # This defines in seconds the length of the downscale stabilization window
    - name: downscaleStabilization
      value: "20"
    # url for fetching the response from jicofo and jibri
    - name: jibriURL
      value: "http://jibri:2222/jibri/api/v1.0/health"
    - name: jicofoURL
      value: "http://jicofo:8888/stats"
```

***config.yaml***
```
evaluate:
  type: "shell"
  timeout: 10000
  shell: 
    entrypoint: "python"
    command: 
      - "/evaluate.py"
metric:
  type: "shell"
  timeout: 10000
  shell: 
    entrypoint: "python"
    command: 
      - "/metric.py"
# "runMode" specifies how often the metric gathering is run, and the information supplied to the metric gathered
# "per-pod" mode runs the metric gatherer against every pod in the resource being managed.
# "per-resource" mode runs the metric gatherer only once against the resource being managed.      
runMode: "per-pod"
# 0 for normal
# 1 for verbose
# 2 - more verbose around high level logic such as autoscaling/rest api
# 3 - more verbose around lower level logic such as metric gathering and evaluation
logVerbosity: 1
# This sets configuration options for the Custom Pod Autoscaler API,
#  allowing enabling and disabling the API and specifying how the API should be exposed
apiConfig:
  enabled: true
  useHTTPS: false
  port: 5000
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  certFile: ""
  keyFile: ""
```

***metrics.py***

```
import json
import sys
import requests
import os
from requests.exceptions import HTTPError

def main():
    # Parse JSON into a dict
    spec = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
    metric(spec)

def metric(spec):
    # get the url from cpa.yaml file
    JIBRI_URL = os.getenv("jibriURL")
    JICOFO_URL = os.getenv("jicofoURL")
    # Get Pod IP
    status = spec["resource"]["status"]
    ip = status["podIP"]
    #print("value of ip is %v", ip)
    try:
        # Make request to jibri Pod metric endpoint
        jibriResponse = requests.get(JIBRI_URL).json()      
        # Make request to jicofo
        jicofoResponse = requests.get(JICOFO_URL).json()

        #  fetching values from jibri and jicofo json data
        jibriStatus = jibriResponse["status"]["busyStatus"]
        jicofoStatusCount = jicofoResponse["jibri_detector"]["count"]
        jicofoStatusAvailable = jicofoResponse["jibri_detector"]["available"]

        # create a json data to pass it to evaluation
        final_response = '{ "busyStatus": "' + jibriStatus + '", "count": "' + str(jicofoStatusCount) + '", "available": "' + str(jicofoStatusAvailable) + '" }' 
        sys.stdout.write(final_response)
        
    except HTTPError as http_err:
        # If an error occurs, output error to stderr and exit with status 1
        sys.stderr.write(f"HTTP error occurred: {http_err}")
        exit(1)
    except Exception as err:
        # If an error occurs, output error to stderr and exit with status 1
        sys.stderr.write(f"Other error occurred: {err}")
        exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

****evaluate.py****

```
# Copyright 2020 The Custom Pod Autoscaler Authors.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import json
import sys
import math
import os

def main():
    # Parse JSON into a dict
    spec = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
    evaluate(spec)

def evaluate(spec):
    # get min and max replicas from cpa.yaml file
    min_replicas = int(os.getenv("minReplicas"))
    max_replicas = int(os.getenv("maxReplicas"))

    # Get current replica count
    target_replica_count = min_replicas

    # Count total available. minimum is 1 replica
    #total_available = min_replicas
    for metric in spec["metrics"]:
        #print("\n metrics is: ", metric)
        json_value = json.loads(metric["value"])
        jibri_status = json_value["busyStatus"]
        jibri_count = int(json_value["count"])
        jibri_available = int(json_value["available"])

        # if single jibri and it's busy or jibri availability is zero then scale
        if jibri_status == "BUSY" and jibri_available == 0 and target_replica_count < max_replicas:
            target_replica_count = target_replica_count + 1
        # if all the jibri's are IDLE and jibri_count is equal to jibri_available then run min replica
        elif jibri_status == "IDLE" and jibri_available == jibri_count and jibri_count > 1:
            target_replica_count = min_replicas

    # Build JSON dict with targetReplicas
    evaluation = {}
    evaluation["targetReplicas"] = target_replica_count

    # Output JSON to stdout
    sys.stdout.write(json.dumps(evaluation))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

```
